t1: We tested Mini-STRC (616-1775) against every relevant cochlear partner in the first pass, then re-ran the decisive TMEM145 multimer on Ultra-Mini (1075-1775) directly. The partner-screen negatives (Piezo2 CED, Otoancorin, Tectorin ZP, TMC1) transfer from Mini-STRC to Ultra-Mini by construction — Ultra-Mini is a strict subset. The TMEM145 positive was the one that needed re-validation on the shorter construct, and it did: see §10 (full-length) and §11 (GOLD-pruned) for the Ultra-Mini × TMEM145 gates. Li et al. (2026) showed that AF3 assembles complexes through interface-level geometric pattern matching learned from training data, not coevolution (bioRxiv). For non-canonical interfaces (membrane-associated, glycan-mediated), AF3 lacks structural priors, producing low ipTM scores even for real interactions — which is why the full-length TMEM145 jobs score low and the GOLD-pruned control scores high.
t2: Key insight: The positive control (NFAT + Calcineurin, ipTM 0.73-0.91) proves our methodology works — and illustrates exactly the mechanism described by Li et al. (2026): AF3 excels at canonical interfaces with strong geometric priors in training data. The negative partner results reflect a distributional mismatch: stereocilin's membrane-associated and glycan-mediated interactions are structurally atypical, analogous to the antigen-antibody limitation Li et al. identified. Low ipTM here is not evidence of non-interaction — it is evidence of AF3's interface prior gap, which is why GOLD-domain pruning (§10) is required for the real TMEM145 interface to resolve.
t18: Also low 0.24 — Ultra-Mini rises to 0.28–0.30 with 94 % C2 (§12)
t24: 5. Partner interaction screen (first-pass Mini-STRC; TMEM145 re-validated on Ultra-Mini)
